verdict_reasoning: EREG is initially expressed as a transmembrane pro-protein, but is rapidly cleaved by ADAM sheddases (especially ADAM17) to release a soluble 46-amino acid mature peptide. While the pro-EREG precursor transiently reaches the cell surface before cleavage, the biologically active mature EREG is secreted into the extracellular space. The protein body of interest (the mature ligand) lacks a membrane anchor and functions as a secreted growth factor. The mature form is not stably surface-anchored via its own mechanism—it is released into solution.
